reasoning: Rockstar Games and Take-Two Interactive have consistently re-affirmed that 'Grand Theft Auto VI' is scheduled for release in the Fall of 2025. They announced the Fall 2025 window in May 2024 and have reiterated it during their August, November, and early February 2025 earnings presentations. If the game launches in 2025, it will certainly have a simultaneous global release in major markets, including Europe, as is standard practice for AAA titles and Rockstar games. However, Rockstar has a strong historical precedent for delaying its major titles (e.g., GTA V and Red Dead Redemption 2 both suffered significant delays). Top industry reporter Jason Schreier has expressed severe skepticism about the 2025 release holding, predicting that the game might slip to early 2026 to avoid extreme developer crunch. It is also worth noting Take-Two's Fiscal Year 2026 (when they expect huge revenue from GTA 6) runs until March 31, 2026, meaning an early 2026 release would still satisfy their fiscal projections while missing the 2025 calendar year for this question's resolution. Given the repeated official reaffirmations, I am leaning strongly towards a 2025 release, but the persistent risk of a 3-6 month slip to early 2026 maintains my probability at ~76%, a bit lower than the 83% community estimate.
